      "それは小さな17\"x22\"のヘックスグリッドマップを持っています。",
      "それは基本的なルールを持っています。",
      "それは100個のカウンターを持っています。",
      "それは8ターンあります。",
      "それは交互のプレイヤーターンの\"I Go, You Go\"システムを持っています。",
      "それは厳格なゾーンコントロールを持っています。",
      "それは粘着性のあるゾーンコントロールを持っています。",
      "それは強制的な戦闘を持っています。"
    ],

      "それは比較的短くてシンプルであることが特徴です。",
      "それは「プレイヤーは通常一晩で勝者を見分けることができる」ことが特徴です。",
      "それは敵ユニットの排除のための勝利ポイントが特徴です。",
      "それはマップの西端からユニットを退出させるための勝利ポイントが特徴です。",
      "それはフランスがゲーム終了時にボード上に残ったプロイセンユニットに対してポイントを獲得することが特徴です。",
      "それはルールがSPIによって1971年に出版されたナポレオン対ウォータールーのために開発されたシステムに基づいていることが特徴です。",
      "それは供給ルールがないことが特徴です。",
      "それは「厳格」と「粘着性」の両方のゾーンコントロールが特徴です。",
      "それは強制的な戦闘が特徴です。",
      "それは戦闘の結果を除いて、このように関与したユニットが互いに離れることができないことが特徴です。"

About: It is about the Battle of Wavre.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Napoleon's Last Battles.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a small 17" x 22" hex grid map, basic rules and 100 counters.
HasA: It has a small 17" x 22" hex grid map.
It has a "I Go, You Go" system of alternating player turns.
It has rigid zones of control.
It has sticky zones of control.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being relatively short and simple.
It is characterized by "players can usually discern the winner in one evening."
It is characterized by victory points for eliminating enemy units.
It is characterized by victory points for exiting units off the western edge of the map.
It is characterized by the French gaining points for any Prussian units left on the board at the end of the game.
It is characterized by the rules being based on the system developed for Napoleon at Waterloo published by SPI in 1971.
It is characterized by no supply rules.
It is characterized by zones of control that are both "rigid" and "sticky".
It is characterized by mandatory combat.
It is characterized by units thus engaged not being able to move away from each other except as a result of combat.
IsA: It is a two-player board wargame.
It is a relatively short and simple game.
KnownAs: It is known as Napoleon's Last Battles.
KnownFor: It is known for simulating the Battle of Wavre.
It is known for being one of four games in the collection Napoleon's Last Battles.
It is known for being released as an individual game.
It is known for its small map size.
It is known for its basic rules.
It is known for its few counters.
It is known for being relatively short and simple.
It is known for its "I Go, You Go" system of alternating player turns.
It is known for its rigid and sticky zones of control.
It is known for its mandatory combat.
PartOf: It is part of Napoleon's Last Battles.
PublishedAs: It was published as one of four games in the popular collection Napoleon's Last Battles.
It was published as an individual game.
It was published as an "folio game".
It was published as a Flemish/French version.
It was published within a new edition of Napoleon's Last Battles.
It was published with revised and streamlined rules and new components.
PublishedBy: It was published by Simulations Publications Inc. (SPI).
It was published by Decision Games.
UsedFor: It is used for simulating the Battle of Wavre.
